reasoning: The FRED series for consumer credit card loans has exhibited a steady upward trend over the last six months (rising from ~1040 in May to 1057 in November). However, the weekly data includes significant volatility, as seen in intermittent dips. The short-term forecast (November 16) favors an increase given the recent strong upward momentum (3 weeks of growth), but this is moderated by weekly variance. Looking further out towards December and February, the linear upward trend suggests continued growth, but uncertainty increases substantially due to horizons crossing the year-end—similar to a major ~36 billion drop observed at the start of January 2025 (likely due to reporting reclassifications). Following instructions, uncertainty increases over time, shifting distant probabilities closer to the 0.5 anchor.
